QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_15

question.text: Who is the regulator of mutual funds in India?

answer.text: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) is the regulator of mutual funds in India. SEBI sets the rules, monitors fund houses, protects investor interests, and ensures transparency in the mutual fund industry. All AMCs and mutual fund schemes must comply with SEBI regulations. **The Trap:** RBI regulates banks, IRDA regulates insurance companies. While the Ministry of Finance oversees the financial sector broadly, SEBI is the direct regulator for mutual funds. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under regulatory framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When clients worry about safety, remind them that mutual funds are one of the most regulated investment products in India. SEBI oversight provides strong investor protection — much stronger than many other investment options.
